purpose: Reconcile the apparent conflict between redteam_summary.json (high_risk_count=3, pass_public_release_gate=false) and release_safety_manifest_v3_17.json (release_safety_gate.status=PASS).

redteam_signal.logic: pass_public_release_gate = (high_risk_count == 0). The scanner is a repo-wide read-only scan; it flags any file under the scanned directories that contains forbidden sorting fields (true_label, oracle_hit) in CSV headers, regardless of whether that file is included in the public release bundle.

release_safety_signal.logic: The artifact ledger classifies each of the 54 tracked artifacts by release safety. release_safety_gate is PASS when (a) no artifact marked safe_to_release=true contains raw text or private sensitive columns, and (b) no private_sensitive artifact appears in the public release bundle.

conflict_explanation.reconciliation: The two signals measure different scopes and are NOT contradictory. The redteam scanner operates repo-wide (including internal-only files) and fails its gate because internal scoring CSVs contain true_label/oracle_hit columns. The release safety manifest operates only on the public release bundle and confirms none of those internal files are included. The conflict is resolved by classifying each high-risk finding and verifying release-bundle exclusion.

high_risk_findings.context_redacted: CSV header contains column 'true_label'. No claim/evidence/raw text is disclosed in this report.
high_risk_findings.release_bundle_verification: Grep of release_bundles/v3_17_confidential_public/MANIFEST.json for 'canonicalized_r4_review_scores' returned no matches. The file is also listed in release_safety_manifest_v3_17.json under private_sensitive (artifact_id A050_review_queue_scores) and contains_raw_text (detected_columns: selected_evidence).
high_risk_findings.why_internal_acceptable: This is an internal scoring artifact used by the canonicalized review queue pipeline. The true_label column is used ONLY for post-hoc metric computation (precision@k, recall@k), never for sorting or routing. The SmartQueue leakage guard (experiments/lightweight_smart_queue_v1/leakage_guard_report.json) confirms labels_used_for_sorting=false and true_label_used_for_metrics_only=true. Keeping true_label in the internal scoring CSV is required for metric reproducibility.
high_risk_findings.why_release_bundle_remains_safe: The build_public_sanitized_release_v1.py script only copies files from explicit inclusion lists (SAFE_SCRIPTS, AGGREGATE_METRIC_FILES, REDACTED_AUDIT_FILES, TOY_EXPERIMENT_DIRS, paper_assets, SAFE_DOCS, configs, tests). canonicalized_review_queue_v1/ is not in any inclusion list. Additionally, has_forbidden_text_columns() would exclude this file because it contains the 'selected_evidence' column. Defense-in-depth: FORBIDDEN_SORTING_COLUMNS check added to build script excludes any CSV with true_label/oracle_hit columns.
high_risk_findings.recommended_action: keep_internal; no change to finding; ensure build script defense-in-depth excludes forbidden sorting columns

high_risk_findings.context_redacted: CSV header contains column 'true_label'. No claim/evidence/raw text is disclosed in this report.
high_risk_findings.release_bundle_verification: Grep of release_bundles/v3_17_confidential_public/MANIFEST.json for 'risk_ranking_features' returned no matches. The file is not listed in any section of release_safety_manifest_v3_17.json (not in safe_to_release, private_sensitive, contains_raw_text, blocked_artifacts, or missing_artifacts). It is an internal analysis artifact not tracked by the artifact ledger.
high_risk_findings.why_internal_acceptable: This is an internal risk-ranking feature matrix used for diagnostic analysis. The true_label column is used ONLY for post-hoc evaluation of ranking quality, never for ranking itself. The risk ranking pipeline sorts by risk_score (a derived feature), not by true_label.
high_risk_findings.why_release_bundle_remains_safe: The build_public_sanitized_release_v1.py script only copies files from explicit inclusion lists. canonicalized_risk_ranking_v1/ is not in any inclusion list. Only risk_ranking_results_by_variant.csv and risk_ranking_report.md (artifact A052, A053) are tracked as safe_to_release. Defense-in-depth: FORBIDDEN_SORTING_COLUMNS check added to build script excludes any CSV with true_label/oracle_hit columns.
high_risk_findings.recommended_action: keep_internal; no change to finding; ensure build script defense-in-depth excludes forbidden sorting columns

high_risk_findings.context_redacted: CSV header contains column 'oracle_hit'. No claim/evidence/raw text is disclosed in this report.
high_risk_findings.release_bundle_verification: Same file as HR-002. Grep of release_bundles/v3_17_confidential_public/MANIFEST.json for 'risk_ranking_features' returned no matches.
high_risk_findings.why_internal_acceptable: This is the same internal risk-ranking feature matrix as HR-002. The oracle_hit column is a diagnostic flag indicating whether the ranking oracle would have selected the same candidate. It is used ONLY for post-hoc evaluation, never for ranking or routing.
high_risk_findings.why_release_bundle_remains_safe: Same as HR-002. The file is not in any inclusion list of build_public_sanitized_release_v1.py. Defense-in-depth: FORBIDDEN_SORTING_COLUMNS check added to build script excludes any CSV with true_label/oracle_hit columns.
high_risk_findings.recommended_action: keep_internal; no change to finding; ensure build script defense-in-depth excludes forbidden sorting columns

release_bundle_safety_confirmation.verification_method: Grep of MANIFEST.json paths for 'canonicalized_r4_review_scores' and 'risk_ranking_features' returned no matches.

final_gate_impact.reconciled_status: WARNING documented — internal-only high-risk findings are classified and excluded from public release. Final Gate should remain WARNING (documented limitation) but NOT FAIL.
final_gate_impact.rationale: The hard constraint is that public release must not contain high-risk files. This is satisfied (0 high-risk files in bundle). The internal high-risk findings are legitimate diagnostic artifacts that use true_label/oracle_hit only for post-hoc metric computation, never for sorting. Removing them would break metric reproducibility. The SmartQueue leakage guard confirms labels_used_for_sorting=false.

defense_in_depth_actions.effect: Any CSV containing these columns is excluded from the public release bundle, even if added to an inclusion list in the future.
